title: Japan's last surviving wooden town 🇯🇵
text: Japan has a problem that uh also afflicted Norway when it comes to the preservation of historical buildings. The favorite building material here was wood just like in my part of the world. Now the problem with wood is that it is uh perishable. So in fire or just you know wear and tear it goes away. So, what you're left with is a bunch of modern buildings that doesn't really capture the feel of what a uh a place looked like historically speaking. But here in the city of Kurashiki, you have buildings that have been here for 200 years, even 300 years. Now, I just walked up here myself. It seems to be a rather quiet area.
